mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and rising near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in more than 60% of walk-forward monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above a restrictive threshold, equity discount rates rise faster than expected earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5% or remains above 3.5% after being below 3.5% within the prior 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate despite continued payroll growth signals late-cycle labor deterioration and margin risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month change remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value is 4.4 and PAYEMS latest snapshot value is 158466
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal has a nonnegative 60-day average forward return in walk-forward tests or if either proxy alone fully explains the return effect.

mechanism_narrative: A break in housing starts below a key activity threshold signals weakening construction demand and lower cyclical earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1500 triggered observations are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum does not deliver higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO momentum over a rolling 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with elevated unemployment identify a restrictive late-cycle regime in which financing costs and labor slack depress risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 3.5% and UNRATE is above 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 3.64 and UNRATE latest snapshot value is 4.4
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-FEDFUNDS high-UNRATE regime has a positive 60-day mean return or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI growth raises the probability of tighter monetary conditions and higher real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change accelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points versus the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside returns occur only outside inflation-sensitive regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with a non-rising policy rate identifies a soft-landing mix where earnings expectations improve without higher discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth is positive and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value is 24065.956 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 3.64
failure_condition: Reject if this joint condition fails to produce above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of walk-forward windows or if positive GDP alone explains the signal.
